---
title: "Veille arXiv"
date: today
date-format: "YYYY-MM-DD"
---

# Veille arXiv

## Catégories surveillées

| Catégorie arXiv | Description | Fréquence de scan |
|-----------------|-------------|-------------------|
| `cs.AI` | Artificial Intelligence | Quotidien |
| `cs.CL` | Computation and Language (NLP) | Quotidien |
| `cs.LG` | Machine Learning | Quotidien |
| `cs.MA` | Multi-Agent Systems | Quotidien |
| `cs.SE` | Software Engineering | Quotidien |
| `stat.ML` | Machine Learning (Stats) | Quotidien |

## Filtres de pertinence

Le script `scripts/veille_arxiv.py` filtre les papers selon :

| Filtre | Mots-clés | Action |
|--------|-----------|--------|
| Agentic | `agent`, `agentic`, `autonomous`, `tool-use` | Priorité haute |
| LLM | `LLM`, `language model`, `transformer`, `GPT`, `Claude` | Priorité haute |
| Sandbox | `sandbox`, `isolation`, `container`, `microVM` | Priorité moyenne |
| RAG | `RAG`, `retrieval augmented`, `GraphRAG`, `knowledge graph` | Priorité haute |
| MCP | `MCP`, `model context protocol` | Priorité haute |
| Harness | `code agent`, `IDE`, `coding assistant` | Priorité moyenne |
| Benchmark | `benchmark`, `evaluation`, `leaderboard` | Priorité moyenne |

## Format de sortie

Chaque paper détecté génère une entrée dans `content/veille/arxiv-feed.qmd` :

```markdown
### [Titre du paper]
- **arXiv** : 2507.17311
- **Date** : 2025-07-23
- **Auteurs** : Intern-AI et al.
- **Catégorie** : cs.AI
- **Tags** : agentic, climate, research
- **Résumé** : Premier self-evolving AI agent pour climate science...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2507.17311
```

## Derniers papers détectés

::: callout-note
## Section auto-générée
Cette section est automatiquement mise à jour par le script `scripts/veille_arxiv.py` via GitHub Actions. Les entrées ci-dessous sont générées à chaque exécution.
:::

<!-- ARXIV_FEED_START -->
### SolarChain-Eval: A Physics-Constrained Benchmark for Trustworthy Economic Agents in Decentralized Energy Markets
- **arXiv** : 2607.08681v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-09
- **Auteurs** : Shilin Ou, Yifan Xu, Luyao Zhang
- **Score** : 11
- **Résumé** : As agentic AI systems are increasingly applied to cyber-physical environments, their evaluation requires assessment of both task performance and trustworthiness. In decentralized energy markets, autonomous agents may improve market utility, but may also exploit invalid physical data, create artifici...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.08681v1

### WebSwarm: Recursive Multi-Agent Orchestration for Deep-and-Wide Web Search
- **arXiv** : 2607.08662v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-09
- **Auteurs** : Xiaoshuai Song, Liancheng Zhang, Kangzhi Zhao, Yutao Zhu, Zhongyuan Wang
- **Score** : 9
- **Résumé** : Large language model (LLM)-based web search agents are transforming information seeking from simple factoid question answering into complex, deep-and-wide search and research-oriented tasks. A single ReAct-style agent is constrained by one long trajectory and limited context, making it difficult to ...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.08662v1

### BiSCo-LLM: Lookup-Free Binary Spherical Coding for Extreme Low-Bit Large Language Model Compression
- **arXiv** : 2607.08643v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-09
- **Auteurs** : Yuantian Shao, Peisong Wang, Zhilei Liu, Chuangyi Li, Yuanteng Chen
- **Score** : 9
- **Résumé** : Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly constrained by memory capacity, weight bandwidth, and checkpoint storage during deployment. Existing low-bit compression methods mainly follow two directions. Scalar or group-wise quantization is simple and compatible with efficient low-precision kernels...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.08643v1

### SMetric: Rethink LLM Scheduling for Serving Agents with Balanced Session-centric Scheduling
- **arXiv** : 2607.08565v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-09
- **Auteurs** : Jiahao Wang, Kaizhan Lin, Kaixi Zhang, Jinbo Han, Xingda Wei
- **Score** : 9
- **Résumé** : LLM scheduling is critical to serving, yet it remains unclear how well existing designs fit agentic serving--with LLM requests issued by agents instead of humans. This shifts the workload in two ways: (1) agents act only on complete responses, making the cluster's tokens per second (TPS) the primary...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.08565v1

### UniClawBench: A Universal Benchmark for Proactive Agents on Real-World Tasks
- **arXiv** : 2607.08768v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-09
- **Auteurs** : Zhekai Chen, Chengqi Duan, Kaiyue Sun, Bohao Li, Yuqing Wang
- **Score** : 7
- **Résumé** : The rapid development of large language models and multimodal large language models has accelerated the emergence of proactive agents capable of operating everyday tools and assisting users in real-world environments. However, existing benchmarks struggle to evaluate such agents effectively, as they...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.08768v1

### AUTOPILOT VQA: Benchmarking Vision-Language Models for Incident-Centric Dashcam Understanding
- **arXiv** : 2607.08745v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-09
- **Auteurs** : Siddharth Damodharan, Radhika Gupta, Ali Alshami, Ryan Rabinowitz, Jugal Kalita
- **Score** : 7
- **Résumé** : Recent advances in Vision-Language Models, Large Language Models, and Multimodal Large Language Models have improved autonomous driving tasks such as scene understanding, decision making, trajectory prediction, and visual question answering. However, evaluating whether these models can reliably reas...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.08745v1

### The Illusion of Equivalency: Statistical Characterization of Quantization Effects in LLMs
- **arXiv** : 2607.08734v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-09
- **Auteurs** : Baha Rababah, Cuneyt Gurcan Akcora, Carson K. Leung
- **Score** : 7
- **Résumé** : Post-training quantization is widely used to deploy large language models in resource-constrained settings, yet its evaluation relies almost exclusively on accuracy and perplexity. We show that these metrics fail to capture behavioral changes induced by quantization. We introduce correctness agreeme...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.08734v1

### The complexities of patient-centred conversational artificial intelligence
- **arXiv** : 2607.08625v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-09
- **Auteurs** : João Matos, Olivia Buege, Donny Cheung, Gary S. Collins, Paula Dhiman
- **Score** : 7
- **Résumé** : Consumer-facing health chatbots powered by large language models (LLMs) are increasingly used for symptom assessment. However, chatbot development and evaluation often rely on cooperative, articulate, simulated patients. We analysed 2,053 real patient-chatbot conversations and found that communicati...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.08625v1

### Workflow as Knowledge: Semantic Persistence for LLM-Mediated Workflows
- **arXiv** : 2607.08740v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-09
- **Auteurs** : Emanuele Quinto, Carlo Andrea Rozzi, Francesco Zanitti
- **Score** : 6
- **Résumé** : Large language model (LLM) applications increasingly use explicit workflows for tool use, retrieval, branching, checkpointing, and human approval. Existing workflow systems already address many execution concerns. This paper proposes a Lisp-inspired but language-independent conceptual model: symboli...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.08740v1

### Validity of LLMs as data annotators: AMALIA on authority
- **arXiv** : 2607.08731v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-09
- **Auteurs** : Manuel Pita
- **Score** : 6
- **Résumé** : A national language model offers a linguistic community its own instrument for measuring what its citizens say and value. Portugal's AMALIA, a publicly funded 9B-parameter model for European Portuguese, appears competitive on agreement alone: asked to code the moral foundation of authority, it agree...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.08731v1

### Resample or Reroute? Budget-Aware Test-Time Model Selection for Large Language Models
- **arXiv** : 2607.08665v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-09
- **Auteurs** : Teng-Ruei Chen
- **Score** : 6
- **Résumé** : Routing among large language models (LLMs) trades response quality against serving cost, motivated by the reported gap between deployed routers and a per-instance oracle. Recent analysis shows that test-time resampling can recover per-instance selection headroom that no single-commit router captures...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.08665v1

### Multi-Modal, Multi-Environment Machine Teaching for Robust Reward Learning
- **arXiv** : 2607.08647v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-09
- **Auteurs** : Ali Larian, Qian Lin, Chang Zong Wu, Daniel S. Brown
- **Score** : 6
- **Résumé** : As autonomous agents are increasingly deployed across diverse operational contexts, aligning their behavior with human intent demands reward functions that remain robust to such changes rather than overfitting to any single environment. Inverse reinforcement learning (IRL) provides a principled way ...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.08647v1

### UltraX: Refining Pre-Training Data at Scale with Adaptive Programmatic Editing
- **arXiv** : 2607.08646v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-09
- **Auteurs** : Xinlong Zhao, Dongsheng Liu, Hengyu Zhao, Zixuan Fu, Zheng Wang
- **Score** : 6
- **Résumé** : As available training data approaches its physical limit, gains from Scaling Laws have begun to diminish. Consequently, improving Large Language Models (LLMs) now depends less on data expansion and more on higher-quality data utilization. However, in the context of large-scale corpora, existing refi...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.08646v1

### Latent Memory Palace: Reasoning for Control as Autoregressive Variational Inference
- **arXiv** : 2607.08724v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-09
- **Auteurs** : Chuning Zhu, Eva Xu, Jose Barreiros, Krishnan Srinivasan, Paarth Shah
- **Score** : 4
- **Résumé** : Human decision-making is highly flexible -- some actions are taken immediately; others require longer deliberation. Language models have exhibited a similar capacity for adaptive "reasoning." However, transferring this capability to continuous control policies has been challenging, as directly reaso...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.08724v1

### Do You Need a Frontier Model as a Citation Verifier? Benchmarking Rubric LLMs for Deep-Research Source Attribution
- **arXiv** : 2607.08700v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-09
- **Auteurs** : Ethan Leung, Elias Lumer, Corey Feld, Austin Huber, Vamse Kumar Subbiah
- **Score** : 4
- **Résumé** : Reinforcement learning increasingly relies on an LLM judge to score each rubric criterion, and that judge acts as the reward model during training. Before such a signal can be trusted, we need to know how capable the judge must be and how biased it is. We study this calibration question for citation...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.08700v1

### DominoTree: Conditional Tree-Structured Drafting with Domino for Speculative Decoding
- **arXiv** : 2607.08642v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-09
- **Auteurs** : Saw S. Lin, Jyh-Shing Roger Jang
- **Score** : 4
- **Résumé** : Speculative decoding accelerates LLM inference by drafting several tokens and verifying them in parallel. Block-diffusion drafters such as DFlash produce a draft block in one pass but model only per-position marginals; best-first tree methods such as DDTree expand candidate trees from those marginal...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.08642v1

### It Takes a MAESTRO To Prune Bad Experts
- **arXiv** : 2607.08601v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-09
- **Auteurs** : Palaash Goel, Ayush Maheshwari, Tanmoy Chakraborty
- **Score** : 4
- **Résumé** : Sparsely-activated Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) language models achieve remarkable inference efficiency by activating only a small fraction of parameters per token, yet their full expert banks reside in memory at all times, creating a prohibitive deployment bottleneck. Existing structured pruning method...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.08601v1

### Score Accuracy Along the Forward Diffusion Does Not Certify Numerical Stability in Diffusion Sampling
- **arXiv** : 2607.08757v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-09
- **Auteurs** : Yiwei Zhou
- **Score** : 3
- **Résumé** : Score matching controls average error under the forward marginals, but a discretized reverse-time sampler evaluates the learned score along its own trajectory. We show that small forward-marginal error does not guarantee numerical stability. We construct a single smooth score field with arbitrarily ...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.08757v1

### Super Weights in LLMs and the Failure of Selective Training
- **arXiv** : 2607.08733v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-09
- **Auteurs** : Shreyas Subramanian, Adewale Akinfaderin, Akarsha Sehwag
- **Score** : 3
- **Résumé** : Recent work identified Super Weights, individual parameters whose removal degrades model performance by orders of magnitude. We show that this degradation due to pruning Super Weights does not universally apply to all LLMs. Furthermore, if these parameters are so important, Super Weight-aware traini...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.08733v1

### Remember When It Matters: Proactive Memory Agent for Long-Horizon Agents
- **arXiv** : 2607.08716v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-09
- **Auteurs** : Yifan Wu, Lizhu Zhang, Yuhang Zhou, Mingyi Wang, Bo Peng
- **Score** : 3
- **Résumé** : In long-horizon tasks, decision-relevant state is often scattered across an expanding trajectory, while the action agent must surface it and act. As trajectories grow, task requirements, environment facts, prior attempts, diagnoses, and open subgoals can be buried in the context window or pushed bey...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.08716v1

<!-- ARXIV_FEED_END -->
